scientific_claim: The study generated population-scale maize sRNAome and transcriptome datasets under drought and well-watered conditions
source_reported_content: The dataset comprises 338 sRNAomes and 197 transcriptomes; 12,476 drought-responsive sRNA traits and 21,757 drought-responsive sRNA cluster traits were detected
study_system: maize diversity panel under WW and DS
limitation: sample handling details require methods check
source_location: Results: Expression variation and genetic control of sRNAs in maize drought response

scientific_claim: Many drought-responsive sRNAs are statistically connected to stress-related gene expression
source_reported_content: Population-level regression uncovered 6,158 significant sRNA-gene coexpression pairs; 1,317 pairs showed negative coexpression; enriched genes included stress/stimulus GO terms and metabolic pathways
limitation: coexpression and target prediction do not prove regulation
source_location: Results: Expression variation and genetic control of sRNAs in maize drought response

scientific_claim: DRESH8 is a drought-stress-specific seQTL hotspot associated with TE-IR-derived sRNAs
source_reported_content: DRESH8 on chromosome 8 was detected only under DS; a ~21.4-kb PAV in ZmPP2C16 intron contained Gypsy TE inverted-repeat arms and was associated with 542 drought-responsive sc-traits and 283 s-traits
method: eGWAS; PAV analysis; LD analysis; TE annotation
causality_level: association before validation; causal after CRISPR evidence
limitation: supplementary methods needed for full eQTL thresholds
source_location: Results: Role and mechanism of DRESH8 in maize drought tolerance

scientific_claim: DRESH8 deletion improves drought survival in maize
source_reported_content: B104 dDRESH8 CRISPR deletion lines had higher survival rates after drought stress than wild-type B104, and DRESH8-derived sRNAs were reduced in dDRESH8
method: CRISPR-Cas9 deletion; drought survival assay; sRNA-seq
limitation: environmental conditions and replicate details require manual PDF check
source_location: Results: Role and mechanism of DRESH8 in maize drought tolerance

scientific_claim: ZmMYBR38 is a validated drought-positive target downstream of DRESH8-derived siRNAs
source_reported_content: mRNA cleavage assays identified ZmMYBR38 as a target of DRESH8-derived sRNAs; ZmMYBR38 overexpression in maize and Arabidopsis increased drought tolerance
method: mRNA cleavage assay; transgenic overexpression; drought assay
limitation: other DRESH8 targets are less deeply validated
source_location: Results: Role and mechanism of DRESH8 in maize drought tolerance

scientific_claim: DRESH8 shows signals consistent with domestication/spread selection
source_reported_content: The paper reports lower nucleotide diversity around DRESH8 in maize than teosinte, Fst/XP-CLR selection signals, no DRESH8 in tested teosinte, and increasing frequency in landraces/modern maize
method: nucleotide diversity; Fst; XP-CLR; allele frequency analysis
source_location: DRESH8 could regulate the trade-off between maize yield and drought tolerance

scientific_claim: TE-IR loci may contribute broadly to drought-yield tradeoffs
source_reported_content: The paper reports DRESH8 association with longer kernels and reduced drought survival, and genome-wide IR-adjacent SNPs associated with both kernel length and survival often had opposite effects
method: genome-wide TE-IR scan; trait association; edited-line phenotyping
data_type: kernel length; survival rate; IR-linked variants
limitation: genome-wide associations need individual validation and field testing
source_location: DRESH8 yield/drought sections; Extended Data captions
notes: Reasonable inference; do not overstate every IR as causal
